project.name: OpenOutreach
project.description: Self-hosted LinkedIn automation for B2B lead generation using Playwright stealth browsing, Bayesian ML pipeline (GPR + BALD), LLM qualification, and Django CRM

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Implements LinkedIn connection request automation using Playwright selectors, supporting direct connect and 'More → Connect' dropdown flows without a note.

nodes.name: send_connection_request
nodes.summary: Public entry point that sends a LinkedIn connection request without a note, returning the new ProfileState (PENDING or QUALIFIED).

nodes.name: message
nodes.summary: Sends LinkedIn messages via direct thread navigation or Voyager Messaging API fallback, with selector chain fallbacks for UI resilience.

nodes.name: send_raw_message
nodes.summary: Public entry point that sends a message to a LinkedIn profile, trying direct thread navigation then Voyager API fallback.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Handles LinkedIn profile navigation and People keyword search, extracting discovered profile URLs and feeding them into the lead enrichment pipeline.

nodes.name: visit_profile
nodes.summary: Navigates to a LinkedIn profile page and triggers discovery/enrichment of any visible profiles on the page.

nodes.name: search_people
nodes.summary: Searches LinkedIn People by keyword, paginates to the requested page, and enriches discovered profile URLs.

nodes.name: status
nodes.summary: Determines LinkedIn connection status for a profile via Voyager API degree lookup with UI button inspection as fallback.

nodes.name: get_connection_status
nodes.summary: Public entry point that returns ProfileState by checking API connection degree first, falling back to UI inspection.

nodes.name: follow_up
nodes.summary: LLM-powered follow-up agent that reads conversation history and profile summaries to produce a structured FollowUpDecision (send_message, mark_completed, or wait).

nodes.name: FollowUpDecision
nodes.summary: Pydantic structured output model for the follow-up agent, encoding action, message, outcome, and follow_up_hours.

nodes.name: run_follow_up_agent
nodes.summary: Public entry point that syncs conversation, renders the Jinja2 prompt, runs the pydantic-ai agent, and returns a FollowUpDecision.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Handles Playwright browser launch with stealth, LinkedIn login (human-typed credentials, comply-gate dismissal), and cookie persistence to DB.

nodes.name: start_browser_session
nodes.summary: Launches the browser with saved cookies or performs a fresh login, saving cookies to DB on success.

nodes.name: launch_browser
nodes.summary: Creates and returns a Playwright page, context, browser, and playwright objects with stealth applied.

nodes.name: playwright_login
nodes.summary: Performs the full LinkedIn login sequence: navigates to login page, types credentials, submits, and waits for feed redirect.

nodes.name: nav
nodes.summary: Core Playwright navigation utilities: goto_page with URL verification, human-typing simulation, top-card finding, and page HTML dumping for diagnostics.

nodes.name: goto_page
nodes.summary: Executes a Playwright action and waits for the expected URL pattern, raising SkipProfile on 404 or RuntimeError on mismatch.

nodes.name: find_top_card
nodes.summary: Locates the LinkedIn profile top-card section element using multiple fallback selectors.

nodes.name: human_type
nodes.summary: Types text into a locator with randomized per-keystroke delay to mimic human input.

nodes.name: registry
nodes.summary: Session registry that maintains a process-wide map of LinkedInProfile pk → AccountSession, plus CLI bootstrap helpers for script entry points.

nodes.name: get_or_create_session
nodes.summary: Returns an existing AccountSession for a LinkedInProfile or creates a new one, keyed by profile pk.

nodes.name: cli_parser
nodes.summary: Bootstraps Django and returns an ArgumentParser with --handle for use in CLI script __main__ blocks.

nodes.name: session
nodes.summary: AccountSession class wrapping a Playwright browser instance, a LinkedInProfile, and a Campaign, with lazy browser launch, cookie refresh, and reauthentication logic.

nodes.name: AccountSession
nodes.summary: Central session object holding Playwright browser state, the LinkedIn profile, campaign context, and cached self-profile; manages browser lifecycle.

nodes.name: conf
nodes.summary: Project-wide configuration constants: paths, browser timeouts, active-hours schedule, planner caps, and campaign ML/timing defaults.

nodes.name: chat
nodes.summary: Syncs LinkedIn conversation messages from the Voyager API into ChatMessage DB rows and incrementally updates the Deal's chat_summary via mem0-style facts.

nodes.name: sync_conversation
nodes.summary: Public entry point: fetches messages from Voyager API, upserts ChatMessage rows, updates chat_summary, and returns messages as dicts.

nodes.name: summaries
nodes.summary: mem0-style fact-list summary pipeline for Deal profile and chat history: extract_facts, update_chat_summary, and reconcile_facts via vendored mem0 UPDATE prompt.

nodes.name: extract_facts
nodes.summary: Calls LLM to extract a flat list of atomic facts about the lead from a text input, with seller identity binding to prevent misattribution.

nodes.name: materialize_profile_summary_if_missing
nodes.summary: Lazily builds Deal.profile_summary on first follow-up touch by scraping the lead's Voyager profile and extracting facts.

nodes.name: update_chat_summary
nodes.summary: Incrementally folds newly-synced ChatMessages into Deal.chat_summary by extracting new facts and reconciling against existing ones.

nodes.name: reconcile_facts
nodes.summary: Merges new facts into an existing fact list using mem0's ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NONE event protocol via LLM.

nodes.name: seller_name_from
nodes.summary: Returns the seller's first name from the session's self_profile with username fallback, used to bind the [Me] identity in LLM prompts.

nodes.name: llm
nodes.summary: LLM model factory and async-to-sync bridge: provides get_llm_model() routing to OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Groq/Mistral/Cohere/openai_compatible, and run_agent_sync() using a persistent worker thread to avoid event-loop conflicts with Playwright.

nodes.name: get_llm_model
nodes.summary: Reads SiteConfig and returns a configured pydantic-ai Model for the configured LLM provider.

nodes.name: run_agent_sync
nodes.summary: Drives a pydantic-ai coroutine to completion from sync code using a dedicated persistent asyncio event loop thread.

nodes.name: _AgentRunner
nodes.summary: Internal singleton that owns a persistent asyncio event loop on a dedicated daemon thread for safe LLM calls alongside synchronous Playwright.

nodes.name: logging
nodes.summary: Centralized logging setup with a compact colored formatter, startup banner printing, and silencing of noisy third-party loggers.

nodes.name: configure_logging
nodes.summary: Configures the root logger with a colored stream handler and silences noisy library loggers.

nodes.name: onboard
nodes.summary: Django management command for running the onboarding wizard interactively or via CLI flags/JSON config file to set up Campaign, account, and LLM config.

nodes.name: rundaemon
nodes.summary: Django management command that migrates DB, ensures onboarding, creates the AccountSession, and starts the main automation daemon loop.

nodes.name: embeddings
nodes.summary: Fastembed text embedding utilities with lazy singleton model loading, producing 384-dim numpy arrays for single or batch text inputs.

nodes.name: embed_text
nodes.summary: Embeds a single text string into a 384-dim float32 numpy array using the lazy-loaded fastembed model.

nodes.name: onboarding
nodes.summary: Onboarding logic: OnboardConfig dataclass, missing_keys() state inspection, collect_from_wizard() interactive TTY wizard, and apply() single idempotent write path for Campaign/Account/LLM DB records.

nodes.name: OnboardConfig
nodes.summary: Pure dataclass holding all values needed to onboard a new user: LinkedIn credentials, campaign info, LLM config, and daily limits.

nodes.name: apply
nodes.summary: Idempotent write path that creates missing Campaign, LinkedInProfile, and SiteConfig records from an OnboardConfig.

nodes.name: missing_keys
nodes.summary: Inspects DB state and returns the set of onboarding field keys that still need values.

nodes.name: search_keywords
nodes.summary: LLM-based generation of LinkedIn People search keywords from campaign product docs and objectives using a Jinja2 prompt template.

nodes.name: generate_search_keywords
nodes.summary: Calls the LLM with a rendered Jinja2 prompt to generate a list of LinkedIn People search keyword strings for a campaign.

nodes.name: self_profile
nodes.summary: Discovers and persists the logged-in user's own LinkedIn profile via Voyager API, creating a disqualified Lead to prevent self-targeting.

nodes.name: discover_self_profile
nodes.summary: Fetches the authenticated user's Voyager profile, upserts a disqualified Lead, caches the URN, and links self_lead on the LinkedInProfile.

nodes.name: tz_detect
nodes.summary: System timezone detection trying Python tzinfo, TZ env var, /etc/timezone, and /etc/localtime symlink sources, with IANA validation and UTC fallback.

nodes.name: system_timezone
nodes.summary: Returns the best-effort system IANA timezone string, falling back to 'UTC' if no valid source is found.

nodes.name: conftest
nodes.summary: Pytest fixtures providing a session-scoped headless Playwright browser, per-test page, and a load_fixture helper for loading saved HTML snapshots.

nodes.name: test_connect_selectors
nodes.summary: Regression tests verifying connect/status/pending Playwright selectors against saved LinkedIn page HTML fixtures, auto-discovering fixture files by category subdirectory.

nodes.name: test_conf
nodes.summary: Django DB tests for get_first_active_profile() covering present, absent, and all-inactive profile cases.

nodes.name: 0004_alter_deal_state
nodes.summary: Django migration that alters the Deal model's state field to use ProfileState enum choices with a default of QUALIFIED.

nodes.name: deals
nodes.summary: Database access layer for Deal operations: state transitions, deal creation, and profile-dict projections used by the pipeline.

nodes.name: leads
nodes.summary: Database access layer for Lead operations: creating enriched leads with embeddings, promoting leads to deals, qualification queries, and URL-slug updates.

nodes.name: enums
nodes.summary: Defines the ProfileState StrEnum representing all states in the lead-to-deal state machine (QUALIFIED through FAILED).

nodes.name: exceptions
nodes.summary: Custom exception classes for authentication errors, terminal state violations, profile skipping, inaccessibility, and connection limit breaches.

nodes.name: models
nodes.summary: Core Django models for the linkedin app: SiteConfig singleton, Campaign, LinkedInProfile with rate-limiting logic, SearchKeyword, ActionLog, and the Task queue model.

nodes.name: ready_pool
nodes.summary: GP confidence gate: promotes QUALIFIED profiles above the Bayesian qualifier threshold to READY_TO_CONNECT, and returns the top-ranked ready candidate.

nodes.name: seeds
nodes.summary: Parses user-supplied seed LinkedIn URLs and creates url-only Leads plus QUALIFIED Deals for them without requiring a browser session.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the linkedin.tasks module.

nodes.name: check_pending
nodes.summary: Task handler that lazily re-checks the oldest due PENDING deal, advancing its state or doubling its backoff if still pending.

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Task handler that resolves a READY_TO_CONNECT candidate at execution time and sends a LinkedIn connection request, handling rate limits and retries.

nodes.name: follow_up
nodes.summary: Task handler that runs the agentic follow-up for the oldest eligible CONNECTED deal, sending approved drafts or generating new messages via the LLM agent.

nodes.name: scheduler
nodes.summary: Per-type 24h Poisson task planner: creates lazy Task slots with working-hours arithmetic, a state-transition hook for PENDING backoff, and a reconcile function for daemon idle cycles.

nodes.name: url_utils
nodes.summary: Utility functions to extract a LinkedIn public_identifier from a profile URL and to reconstruct a canonical profile URL from a public_identifier.

nodes.name: test_profiles
nodes.summary: Tests for url_utils, lead creation/enrichment, deal promotion, qualification queries, state transitions, and multi-campaign scoping in the db layer.

nodes.name: test_tasks
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the three task handlers (connect, check_pending, follow_up) covering state transitions, rate limits, and lazy slot behavior.

nodes.name: test_action_log
nodes.summary: Tests for LinkedInProfile.can_execute, record_action, mark_exhausted, and daily rate-limit logic using ActionLog.

nodes.name: test_ready_pool
nodes.summary: Tests for the ready_pool GP gate: promote_to_ready threshold behavior, cold-start, and find_ready_candidate ranking.

nodes.name: test_reconcile
nodes.summary: Tests for the scheduler reconcile function: stale task recovery, per-type slot planning, and idempotency.

nodes.name: test_scheduler_advanced
nodes.summary: Advanced tests for Poisson slot timing, active-hours arithmetic, on_deal_state_entered hook, and plan_*_window planners.

nodes.name: ProfileState
nodes.summary: StrEnum defining the six states of the lead-deal state machine: QUALIFIED, READY_TO_CONNECT, PENDING, CONNECTED, COMPLETED, FAILED.

nodes.name: AuthenticationError
nodes.summary: Raised on 401 Unauthorized; triggers session reauthentication in the daemon.

nodes.name: ReachedConnectionLimit
nodes.summary: Raised when LinkedIn's weekly connection limit is reached; marks the action type exhausted.

nodes.name: SkipProfile
nodes.summary: Raised when a profile must be skipped during processing.

nodes.name: ProfileInaccessibleError
nodes.summary: Raised for private, deleted, or restricted profiles (403/404); causes the deal to be marked FAILED.

nodes.name: SiteConfig
nodes.summary: Singleton DB model holding global site config: LLM provider, API key, model name, and API base URL.

nodes.name: Campaign
nodes.summary: Campaign model with product docs, objective, booking link, message-approval flag, and a binary ML model blob.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfile
nodes.summary: Stores LinkedIn credentials and daily rate-limit state for a Django User; exposes can_execute/record_action/mark_exhausted for rate enforcement.

nodes.name: SearchKeyword
nodes.summary: Per-campaign search keywords with usage tracking.

nodes.name: ActionLog
nodes.summary: Immutable log of connect/follow_up actions per LinkedInProfile and Campaign, used to enforce daily rate limits.

nodes.name: Task
nodes.summary: Persistent task queue row with task_type, status lifecycle (PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED), scheduled_at, and a minimal campaign_id payload.

nodes.name: set_profile_state
nodes.summary: Central state-transition function: moves a Deal to a new ProfileState, persists reason/outcome, logs the change, and fires the scheduler hook.

nodes.name: create_disqualified_deal
nodes.summary: Creates a FAILED Deal with WRONG_FIT outcome for an LLM-rejected lead (campaign-scoped, not a permanent account disqualification).

nodes.name: create_enriched_lead
nodes.summary: Creates a Lead row from a Voyager profile dict, caches the URN, and triggers embedding generation. Returns None on duplicates.

nodes.name: discover_and_enrich
nodes.summary: Drives batch profile discovery: filters new URLs, calls Voyager API for each, and creates enriched leads with human-like delays.

nodes.name: promote_lead_to_deal
nodes.summary: Creates a QUALIFIED Deal for an existing Lead in the current campaign.

nodes.name: promote_to_ready
nodes.summary: Fetches embeddings for all QUALIFIED profiles and promotes those above the GP confidence threshold to READY_TO_CONNECT.

nodes.name: find_ready_candidate
nodes.summary: Returns the top-ranked READY_TO_CONNECT profile according to the BayesianQualifier, or None if pool is empty.

nodes.name: reconcile
nodes.summary: Daemon idle-cycle entry point: recovers stale RUNNING tasks and re-plans each (campaign, task_type) whose pending queue is empty.

nodes.name: poisson_slot_times
nodes.summary: Returns n strictly-increasing timestamps inside the working portion of the 24h horizon using the order-statistic representation of a Poisson process.

nodes.name: on_deal_state_entered
nodes.summary: State-transition hook: stamps next_check_pending_at on PENDING deals using the current backoff; no-op for all other states.

nodes.name: working_seconds_in_window
nodes.summary: Computes total working seconds between two datetimes, respecting active-hours boundaries when ENABLE_ACTIVE_HOURS is True.

nodes.name: handle_connect
nodes.summary: Task handler entry point: resolves a candidate, checks connection status, sends a request, and transitions deal state with full error handling.

nodes.name: handle_check_pending
nodes.summary: Task handler entry point: picks the oldest due PENDING deal, checks its status, and either advances state or doubles the backoff.

nodes.name: handle_follow_up
nodes.summary: Task handler entry point: sends any pre-approved draft first, then runs the LLM follow-up agent for the next eligible CONNECTED deal.

nodes.name: url_to_public_id
nodes.summary: Extracts the percent-decoded public identifier from a LinkedIn /in/ profile URL; returns None for non-profile URLs.

nodes.name: public_id_to_url
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a canonical LinkedIn profile URL from a public_identifier, percent-encoding special characters.

nodes.name: create_seed_leads
nodes.summary: Creates url-only Lead rows plus QUALIFIED Deals for seed public IDs without requiring a browser session.

nodes.name: ConnectStrategy
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling the candidate-finder callable, optional pre-connect hook, and qualifier for a connect task execution.

nodes.name: TaskQuerySet
nodes.summary: Custom QuerySet for Task: adds pending(), claim_next(), and seconds_to_next() helpers used by the daemon loop.

nodes.name: conversations
nodes.summary: High-level action layer for retrieving LinkedIn conversations: finds conversation URNs via API or browser navigation fallback, parses raw Voyager message elements into clean dicts, and provides a top-level get_conversation() entry point.

nodes.name: profile
nodes.summary: Action layer for scraping a LinkedIn profile via the Voyager API, with an optional fixture-save helper for test data collection.

nodes.name: client
nodes.summary: Core authenticated Voyager API client: runs fetch() inside the Playwright browser page so requests carry all real browser cookies and headers, with retry logic and error handling for profile and connection-degree endpoints.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Public re-export surface for the messaging API subpackage, exposing encode_urn, check_response, send_message, create_conversation, fetch_conversations, and fetch_messages as a flat namespace.

nodes.name: conversations
nodes.summary: Fetches conversations and messages from the LinkedIn Voyager GraphQL messaging endpoint using hardcoded queryIds, with tenacity retry on IOError.

nodes.name: send
nodes.summary: Sends messages to existing LinkedIn conversations and creates new conversation threads via the Voyager messaging REST endpoints, with UUID origin tokens for deduplication and tenacity retries.

nodes.name: utils
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for messaging API modules: URN percent-encoding for Voyager GraphQL variables and HTTP response status checking with AuthenticationError on 401.

nodes.name: voyager
nodes.summary: Pure parsing layer for LinkedIn Voyager API responses: dataclass definitions for profiles, positions, and educations, plus parse_linkedin_voyager_response() and parse_connection_degree() that extract clean dicts from raw JSON with embedded URN-reference resolution.

nodes.name: test_voyager
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pytest suite for parse_linkedin_voyager_response(): covers full-profile selection among mixed entity types, geo/location resolution fallback paths, positions/educations URN-chain traversal, connection degree parsing, industry resolution, and JSON serializability.

nodes.name: PlaywrightLinkedinAPI
nodes.summary: Authenticated Voyager API client that runs fetch() inside the Playwright browser page context. Extracts JSESSIONID from cookies for CSRF token, provides get/post methods, and implements get_profile() and get_connection_degree() with exponential-backoff retry.

nodes.name: _FetchResponse
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the dict returned by page.evaluate(fetch(...)), exposing status, ok, json(), and text() surface.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfile
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a complete parsed LinkedIn profile including name, headline, summary, positions, educations, geo, connection distance/degree, and supported locales.

nodes.name: Position
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a LinkedIn work position with title, company, location, date range, and description.

nodes.name: Education
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a LinkedIn education entry with school name, degree, field of study, and date range.

nodes.name: parse_linkedin_voyager_response
nodes.summary: Parses a full Voyager API JSON response into a clean serializable dict: resolves URN references, selects the FullProfile entity among MiniProfiles, builds position/education chains, and extracts geo and connection info.

nodes.name: parse_connection_degree
nodes.summary: Extracts connection degree (1/2/3/None) by scanning included MemberRelationship entities from any Voyager decoration response.

nodes.name: get_conversation
nodes.summary: Top-level action that retrieves all past messages with a LinkedIn profile: tries API-based conversation lookup first, falls back to browser navigation capture, then parses and returns sorted message dicts.

nodes.name: find_conversation_urn
nodes.summary: Finds a conversation URN by scanning the first page of recent conversations from the API for a matching participant URN.

nodes.name: find_conversation_urn_via_navigation
nodes.summary: Fallback method that navigates the browser to the messaging thread URL and intercepts the messengerMessages response to capture the conversation URN for older threads not in API page 1.

nodes.name: parse_messages
nodes.summary: Parses raw Voyager messages response into a sorted list of {sender, text, timestamp} dicts, skipping elements without body text.

nodes.name: scrape_profile
nodes.summary: Ensures browser session, creates a PlaywrightLinkedinAPI instance, calls get_profile(), and returns the (profile, raw_data) tuple.

nodes.name: fetch_conversations
nodes.summary: Fetches recent conversations list from Voyager GraphQL endpoint using messengerConversations queryId, with retry logic.

nodes.name: fetch_messages
nodes.summary: Fetches messages for a specific conversation URN from Voyager GraphQL endpoint using messengerMessages queryId, with retry logic.

nodes.name: send_message
nodes.summary: Sends a message to an existing conversation via Voyager createMessage action endpoint, generating a UUID origin token for deduplication and logging delivery confirmation.

nodes.name: create_conversation
nodes.summary: Creates a brand-new conversation thread with a target profile using the legacy Voyager messaging/conversations endpoint, sending the first message in the same request.

nodes.name: encode_urn
nodes.summary: Percent-encodes a URN string for safe embedding inside Voyager GraphQL variable expressions.

nodes.name: check_response
nodes.summary: Validates a messaging API response, raising AuthenticationError on 401 and IOError on other non-OK statuses.

nodes.name: get_profile
nodes.summary: Calls the Voyager /identity/dash/profiles endpoint with FullProfileWithEntities decoration, validates the response, and returns a parsed profile dict plus raw JSON.

nodes.name: get_connection_degree
nodes.summary: Fetches connection degree using the lightweight TopCardSupplementary decoration which reliably includes MemberRelationship entities.

nodes.name: voyager_identity_dash_profiles
nodes.summary: GET /voyager/api/identity/dash/profiles — Voyager profile endpoint used with both FullProfileWithEntities-91 and TopCardSupplementary-120 decorations.

nodes.name: voyager_graphql_messenger_conversations
nodes.summary: GET /voyager/api/voyagerMessagingGraphQL/graphql?queryId=messengerConversations.* — fetches paginated list of recent conversations.

nodes.name: voyager_graphql_messenger_messages
nodes.summary: GET /voyager/api/voyagerMessagingGraphQL/graphql?queryId=messengerMessages.* — fetches messages for a specific conversation URN.

nodes.name: voyager_create_message
nodes.summary: POST /voyager/api/voyagerMessagingDashMessengerMessages?action=createMessage — sends a message to an existing conversation thread.

nodes.name: voyager_create_conversation
nodes.summary: POST /voyager/api/messaging/conversations — legacy endpoint to create a new conversation thread with first message.

nodes.name: qualifier
nodes.summary: Implements the BayesianQualifier using Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) with BALD active learning for lead qualification. Provides a Qualifier protocol, LLM-based qualification via pydantic-ai, probability utilities, and the full GPR pipeline including lazy fitting, class balancing, BALD acquisition, and persistence to the Campaign.model_blob DB field.

nodes.name: pools
nodes.summary: Coordinates the lazy pipeline of composable generators: search_source → qualify_source → ready_source → find_candidate. Implements adaptive exploit/explore threshold logic to decide when to search for new profiles vs. qualify existing candidates.

nodes.name: qualify
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the qualification step: fetches candidates with embeddings, selects the best one via BALD/acquisition scores, calls the LLM qualifier, and persists the result as a qualified Deal or disqualified Deal.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Manages LinkedIn People search: selects the next unused SearchKeyword (generating fresh ones via LLM if needed) and calls search_people to discover new leads.

nodes.name: test_qualifier
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for BayesianQualifier covering update, predict, BALD scores, rank_profiles, warm_start, pool_has_targets, and explain methods.

nodes.name: test_pools
nodes.summary: Tests for the pool pipeline generators (_needs_search, search_source, qualify_source, ready_source, find_candidate), covering exploit/explore mode, adaptive threshold, and exhaustion scenarios.

nodes.name: test_qualify
nodes.summary: Tests for run_qualification covering LLM always-called behavior, cold-start behavior, and disqualification on promotion failure.

nodes.name: Qualifier
nodes.summary: Runtime-checkable Protocol defining the shared interface for all qualifier implementations (rank_profiles and explain methods).

nodes.name: QualificationDecision
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseModel for structured LLM output: qualified (bool) and reason (str).

nodes.name: BayesianQualifier
nodes.summary: Core ML component implementing GPR-based active learning qualification. Manages an sklearn StandardScaler+GPR pipeline with lazy fitting, class balancing (max 2:1 imbalance), BALD acquisition, predict_probs, acquisition_scores, rank_profiles, explain, and warm_start. Persists the fitted pipeline to Campaign.model_blob.

nodes.name: qualify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Calls the configured LLM via pydantic-ai to qualify a lead profile using a Jinja2 prompt template. Returns (label, reason) where label is 1=accept or 0=reject.

nodes.name: format_prediction
nodes.summary: Returns a compact one-liner stats string for qualification logging: P(f>0.5), entropy, std, and observation count.

nodes.name: find_candidate
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point for regular campaigns: returns the top profile ready for connection by consuming one item from ready_source, or None if the pipeline is exhausted.

nodes.name: ready_source
nodes.summary: Generator that yields ready-to-connect candidates by finding existing ready candidates, promoting qualified ones via promote_to_ready, or pulling a new qualification from qualify_source when the ready pool is empty.

nodes.name: qualify_source
nodes.summary: Generator that yields public_ids from run_qualification, pulling from search_source when in exploit mode with an empty positive pool. Each yield produces one label that shifts the GP model.

nodes.name: search_source
nodes.summary: Generator that yields keywords from run_search, stopping when run_search returns None (all keywords exhausted).

nodes.name: _needs_search
nodes.summary: Determines whether a new LinkedIn search is needed: returns True only in exploit mode when no unlabeled candidate meets the adaptive probability threshold (base - 1/sqrt(n_obs)). Returns False on cold start, explore mode, empty pool, or degenerate GP predictions.

nodes.name: run_qualification
nodes.summary: Selects the best unqualified candidate via BALD/acquisition scores, fetches its profile text, calls qualify_with_llm, and persists the result (qualified Deal or disqualified Deal). Returns the public_id or None.

nodes.name: fetch_qualification_candidates
nodes.summary: Returns Lead rows with embeddings awaiting qualification for the current campaign session, with a fallback to trigger embedding computation for any leads that were missed at discovery time.

nodes.name: run_search
nodes.summary: Selects the next unused SearchKeyword for the campaign (generating fresh keywords via LLM if none remain), marks it used, and calls search_people to discover new LinkedIn profiles.

nodes.name: models
nodes.summary: Defines the ChatMessage model for storing LinkedIn conversation messages attached to any Django model via a GenericForeignKey. Tracks direction (outgoing/incoming), LinkedIn URN for dedup, threading, and recipients.

nodes.name: admin
nodes.summary: Django Admin registrations for Lead and Deal models, featuring colored state/outcome badges, chat bubble renderer, conversation thread inline, and profile/chat summary displays. Provides the primary CRM UI for monitoring outreach campaigns.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Package init that re-exports Lead, Deal, and Outcome from their respective submodules for convenient imports.

nodes.name: deal
nodes.summary: Defines the Outcome TextChoices enum and the Deal model which tracks a lead's progression through a campaign pipeline, including state, outcome, LLM-generated summaries, pending message drafts, and scheduling fields.

nodes.name: lead
nodes.summary: Defines the Lead model storing a LinkedIn prospect's URL, public identifier, URN, and a lazily-computed 384-dim embedding. Provides lazy accessor methods for live Voyager profile scraping, URN resolution, embedding computation, and labeled array extraction for ML warm-start.

nodes.name: admin
nodes.summary: Django Admin registrations for all core linkedin-app models: SiteConfig, Campaign (with pipeline state pills), LinkedInProfile, SearchKeyword, ActionLog, Task, and ChatMessage with conversation thread rendering.

nodes.name: ChatMessage
nodes.summary: Stores individual LinkedIn conversation messages with a GenericForeignKey to the owning object (typically a Lead), direction flag, LinkedIn URN for deduplication, threading via self-references, and creation date.

nodes.name: Deal
nodes.summary: Tracks a single lead's engagement within a campaign: pipeline state, outcome, LLM-generated profile and chat summaries stored as JSON, pending message draft with approval flag, connection attempt counters, and scheduling timestamps.

nodes.name: Lead
nodes.summary: Stores a LinkedIn prospect's identity (URL, public_identifier, URN) and a binary-encoded 384-dim FastEmbed embedding. Disqualification flag permanently excludes a lead from all campaigns.

nodes.name: ChatMessageInline
nodes.summary: GenericTabularInline that renders read-only conversation messages with direction indicators on the LeadAdmin change page.

nodes.name: LeadAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin view for Lead with clickable LinkedIn links, deal count annotation, embedding presence indicator, and ChatMessageInline for conversation history.

nodes.name: DealAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin view for Deal with colored state/outcome badges, idle-days coloring, message count link, profile/chat summary fact lists, and full conversation thread bubble renderer.

nodes.name: SiteConfigAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for the SiteConfig singleton; prevents creation of a second instance and disallows deletion.

nodes.name: CampaignAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for Campaign with an annotated pipeline column showing per-state deal counts as colored pills.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfileAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for LinkedInProfile (the automation account), exposing username, contact email, active flag, and legal acceptance.

nodes.name: SearchKeywordAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for SearchKeyword entries tied to campaigns, showing usage status and timestamp.

nodes.name: ActionLogAdmin
nodes.summary: Read-only admin for ActionLog entries recording LinkedIn actions (connects, messages) by profile and campaign.

nodes.name: TaskAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for Task queue entries with read-only fields and a computed campaign name resolved from the JSON payload.

nodes.name: ChatMessageAdmin
nodes.summary: Admin for ChatMessage with conversation thread bubble renderer, participants column, and direction display; imports _render_chat_thread from crm.admin.

nodes.name: setup_crm
nodes.summary: Django management command that exposes the `setup_crm` bootstrap function as a runnable CLI command. Thin wrapper around `linkedin/management/setup_crm.py`.

nodes.name: setup_crm
nodes.summary: Idempotent CRM bootstrap script that ensures the default Django `Site` object (id=1, domain=localhost) exists. Called at test startup and via the management command.

nodes.name: test_follow_up
nodes.summary: Tests for the follow-up agent's system-prompt renderer and recent-message loader, verifying that profile/chat summaries appear correctly and that the message window is respected.

nodes.name: conftest
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration providing autouse fixtures for CRM bootstrap and embedding stubs, plus a `fake_session` fixture that supplies a lightweight `AccountSession` stand-in backed by the test database.

nodes.name: test_summaries
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for the mem0-style fact-list boundary in `linkedin/db/summaries.py`, covering `extract_facts`, `materialize_profile_summary_if_missing`, `update_chat_summary`, and `reconcile_facts` with LLM-mocking helpers.

nodes.name: factories
nodes.summary: Factory-Boy model factories for `User`, `Lead`, and `Deal` used across the test suite to create minimal, realistic DB fixtures.

nodes.name: Command.handle
nodes.summary: Django management command handler; delegates to `setup_crm()` with no extra logic.

nodes.name: setup_crm
nodes.summary: Ensures the default Django `Site` record (id=1) exists; idempotent and safe to call at any time.

nodes.name: FakeAccountSession
nodes.summary: Minimal AccountSession stand-in exposing `django_user`, `campaign`, `self_profile`, `campaigns` property, and a no-op `ensure_browser` for unit tests.

nodes.name: fake_session
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture that creates a User, Campaign, and LinkedInProfile in the test DB and returns a FakeAccountSession wrapping them.

nodes.name: _ensure_crm_data
nodes.summary: Autouse fixture that calls `setup_crm()` before every test to guarantee the default Site row exists.

nodes.name: _mock_embeddings
nodes.summary: Autouse fixture that stubs `embed_text` to return a constant numpy array, preventing ONNX model loads in tests unless the `no_embed_mock` marker is set.

nodes.name: UserFactory
nodes.summary: Factory-Boy factory for Django's built-in `User` model with random usernames, staff and active flags set.

nodes.name: LeadFactory
nodes.summary: Factory-Boy factory for the `crm.Lead` model generating sequential public identifiers and matching LinkedIn URLs.

nodes.name: DealFactory
nodes.summary: Factory-Boy factory for the `crm.Deal` model with an auto-created Lead sub-factory.

nodes.name: TestExtractFacts
nodes.summary: Test class covering `extract_facts`: empty-input guard, LLM invocation, structured-output parsing, and seller-name identity binding in the system prompt.

nodes.name: TestMaterializeProfileSummary
nodes.summary: Test class for `materialize_profile_summary_if_missing`: verifies noop when already built, re-scrape path, and graceful handling of empty profile data.

nodes.name: TestUpdateChatSummary
nodes.summary: Test class for `update_chat_summary`: covers noop on empty messages, seller-only burst short-circuit, first-pass extraction, second-pass reconciliation, and blank-message filtering.

nodes.name: TestReconcileFacts
nodes.summary: Test class for `reconcile_facts`: covers ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NONE LLM events, hallucinated-id skipping, markdown-wrapped JSON fallback, and reasoning-model think-block stripping.

nodes.name: TestRenderSystemPrompt
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that `_render_system_prompt` includes profile-summary facts, chat-summary facts, verbatim Me:/Lead: messages, and gracefully handles missing summaries.

nodes.name: TestLoadRecentMessages
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that `_load_recent_messages` respects the `RECENT_MESSAGES_WINDOW` limit and returns messages in chronological order.

nodes.name: daemon
nodes.summary: Core daemon run loop that drives all LinkedIn automation: claims tasks from the queue, dispatches them to typed handlers, manages active-hours scheduling, handles authentication failures and browser crashes, and runs the scheduler reconciliation loop on idle. Also provides human-rhythm pacing (burst/break) and heartbeat logging for long idle sleeps.

nodes.name: diagnostics
nodes.summary: Captures post-mortem debug artifacts (page HTML, screenshot, error traceback) into a timestamped folder on automation failures. Exposes a `failure_diagnostics` context manager used by the daemon to wrap task execution.

nodes.name: test_schedule
nodes.summary: Pytest test suite for the `seconds_until_active` function in daemon.py, verifying correct active-window detection across inside/outside window, boundary conditions, timezone handling, and weekend behavior (no weekend skip).

nodes.name: Heartbeat
nodes.summary: Rate-limited logger that emits an 'alive' line at most once per interval (default 5 minutes), used to confirm the daemon is running during long idle sleeps.

nodes.name: _HumanRhythmBreak
nodes.summary: Wall-clock burst timer that injects randomized breaks between work bursts to mimic human activity patterns and reduce LinkedIn detection risk.

nodes.name: run_daemon
nodes.summary: Main daemon loop: initializes qualifiers and campaigns, then loops indefinitely — enforcing active hours, claiming tasks, dispatching handlers, and reconciling the scheduler on idle. Handles TargetClosedError, AuthenticationError, and ModelHTTPError specially.

nodes.name: seconds_until_active
nodes.summary: Returns seconds until the next active working window opens, or 0 if currently active. Respects ENABLE_ACTIVE_HOURS flag and a single contiguous daily window with no weekend skip.

nodes.name: sleep_with_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Sleeps for a given duration while waking every HEARTBEAT_SLICE seconds to allow the Heartbeat to fire, preventing silent gaps longer than 5 minutes.

nodes.name: _build_qualifiers
nodes.summary: Instantiates a BayesianQualifier per campaign and warm-starts each with existing labeled Lead data from the database.

nodes.name: capture_failure
nodes.summary: Saves a traceback text file, page HTML, and screenshot into a timestamped folder under DIAGNOSTICS_DIR for post-mortem debugging of automation failures.

nodes.name: failure_diagnostics
nodes.summary: Context manager that wraps a block, calls capture_failure on any unhandled exception, and re-raises it. Used by the daemon to instrument task handler execution.

nodes.name: TestSecondsUntilActive
nodes.summary: Pytest test class with 9 parameterized cases covering seconds_until_active: inside window, before start, after end, exact boundaries, weekend (no skip), timezone offset, and feature-flag disabled.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the OpenOutreach LinkedIn automation service: a deps stage installs Python packages via uv, and a runtime stage installs Playwright Chromium, an optional VNC/noVNC stack, and sets up the non-root ubuntu user. Exposes entrypoint and start scripts for container orchestration.

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs the test suite and, on success, builds and pushes the Docker image to ghcr.io/eracle/openoutreach with latest, SHA, and semver tags. Also triggers a downstream DigitalOcean snapshot rebuild via repository dispatch.

nodes.name: tests
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full pytest test suite inside Docker on every push to main and on all pull requests, using `make docker-test`.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion file that strips development artifacts (.git, .github, .venv, caches, IDE dirs) from the image to keep builds clean and fast.

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE
nodes.summary: Comprehensive module-level architecture reference for OpenOutreach, covering entry flow, state machine, task queue, ML pipeline, Django apps, CRM data model, key modules, configuration, Docker, CI/CD, and dependencies. Companion to CLAUDE.md, intended as the deep-dive technical reference for contributors.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project rules and quick-reference guide for Claude Code (AI assistant), covering Python env conventions, commit style, dependency management, error handling policy, architecture overview, and CLI command reference.

nodes.name: LEGAL_NOTICE
nodes.summary: Legal notice disclosing that LinkedIn automation violates LinkedIn's ToS, explaining the freemium promotional action mechanism, automatic newsletter subscription behaviour for non-GDPR jurisdictions, and liability disclaimers. Acceptance is required before use.

nodes.name: LICENCE
nodes.summary: Full text of the GNU General Public License v3, under which OpenOutreach is distributed.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Top-level automation Makefile providing targets for local dev (install, setup, run, admin, test) and Docker workflows (build, up, up-view, stop, logs, docker-test). Acts as the primary developer interface for all common project operations.

nodes.name: README
nodes.summary: Main project README describing OpenOutreach's value proposition, quick-start Docker and local dev instructions, full oo CLI reference, ML pipeline explanation, project structure, and links to documentation, community, and sponsorship.

nodes.name: local.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose file for local development: builds the app service from the project Dockerfile with BUILD_ENV=local, mounts the entire repo into /app, enables VNC, and exposes noVNC on port 6080.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration declaring the openoutreach-local package (Python 3.12+), the oo CLI entry point mapping to oo_cli:app, and setuptools as the build backend.

nodes.name: pytest.ini
nodes.summary: pytest configuration setting the Python path to the project root, pointing the test runner at the tests/ directory, setting DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE, and defining the no_embed_mock marker to skip the autouse fastembed mock.

nodes.name: metadata
nodes.summary: Conductor track metadata for the ops_health_20260528 track, recording its type (feature), status (new), timestamps, and a description of the goal: getting the local Python runtime fully operational from a clean state.

nodes.name: plan
nodes.summary: Phased execution plan for the ops_health_20260528 Conductor track, listing completed tasks for environment setup (venv, deps, Playwright browsers, migrations, CRM bootstrap) and runtime verification (tests, Django Admin, daemon).

nodes.name: spec
nodes.summary: Specification for the ops_health_20260528 track defining the goal (fully operational local runtime), scope (venv, browsers, migrations, CRM, tests, admin, daemon), and success criteria with no new features or refactoring in scope.

nodes.name: product-guidelines
nodes.summary: Defines tone, voice, messaging guidelines, visual identity, and ethical principles for OpenOutreach, covering LLM-generated outreach messages, Django Admin copy, and operator responsibilities.

nodes.name: product
nodes.summary: Product guide describing the vision, target users (founders, sales teams, agencies), core value propositions (autonomous lead discovery, undetectable operation, data ownership), key features, and success metrics for OpenOutreach.

nodes.name: setup_state
nodes.summary: Conductor setup state file tracking the last successfully completed Conductor initialization step.

nodes.name: tech-stack
nodes.summary: Documents the full technology stack for OpenOutreach, covering Python/Django, Playwright browser automation, scikit-learn/fastembed ML, pydantic-ai LLM integration, SQLite, Jinja2 templating, custom task queue, pytest, Docker, and GitHub Actions CI/CD.

nodes.name: tracks
nodes.summary: Conductor tracks registry file listing all major project tracks; currently empty (no active tracks listed).

nodes.name: workflow
nodes.summary: Defines the Conductor development workflow, including task lifecycle (select → implement → commit → git-notes → checkpoint), quality gates, commit message format, definition of done, and emergency procedures.

nodes.name: GDPR_ROADMAP
nodes.summary: Detailed GDPR compliance implementation roadmap organized into workstreams (differential privacy embeddings, credential encryption, PII cleanup, data retention, right to erasure, stored name removal, and data portability), with specific file-level changes for each workstream.

nodes.name: architecture
nodes.summary: Comprehensive system architecture reference covering all major components: data models, state machine, daemon task queue, task handlers, pipeline (qualification, search, pools), API client, browser automation, actions, DB operations, agents, ML qualification, and error handling.

nodes.name: configuration
nodes.summary: Configuration reference covering LLM settings (.env), Campaign and Account Django model fields, hardcoded timing and ML defaults in conf.py, and GDPR-aware newsletter subscription logic.

nodes.name: default_campaign
nodes.summary: Defines the ideal customer profile (indie hacker/bootstrapped founder) and LinkedIn search keyword strategy for OpenOutreach's own lead-generation campaign, including job titles, interests, and pain-point keywords.

nodes.name: docker
nodes.summary: Docker installation and usage guide covering the pre-built GHCR image quick-start, available tags, VNC setup, stopping and restarting, and Docker Compose build-from-source workflow with useful make commands.

nodes.name: follow_up_agent
nodes.summary: Full documentation for the follow-up agent including the pipeline overview, FollowUpDecision structured output schema, agent context sections, summaries pipeline (profile and chat), conversation sync, message-sending strategies, scheduling and deduplication, rate limiting, failure handling, and the Mom Test prompt strategy.

nodes.name: playwright-linkedin-roadmap
nodes.summary: Roadmap for extracting the LinkedIn browser/API modules into a standalone pip-installable `playwright-linkedin` library, with proposed Python API and CLI interfaces, a table of what moves vs stays, coupling cuts already completed, and remaining steps.

nodes.name: profile_lifecycle
nodes.summary: End-to-end documentation of the lead profile lifecycle from discovery through enrichment, qualification, GP confidence gate, connection, check-pending, follow-up, and terminal states, with state diagram and freemium campaign variations.

nodes.name: remove_profile_dict
nodes.summary: Refactoring spec for removing the `to_profile_dict()` intermediate dict pattern and replacing it with direct Lead/Deal model instances throughout qualifiers, pools, and task handlers.

nodes.name: template-variables
nodes.summary: Reference for all variables available in the follow-up agent's Jinja2 system prompt template and the full Voyager API profile data structure (top-level fields, positions, educations, date ranges, geo, industry).

nodes.name: templating
nodes.summary: Overview of the follow-up messaging system explaining how the LLM agent generates messages, the configuration fields (campaign product_docs, campaign_objective, booking_link, LLM settings), and a reference to the template variables doc.

nodes.name: testing
nodes.summary: Testing guide covering the pytest framework setup, how to run tests (local, single file, Docker), pytest.ini configuration, the autouse CRM setup fixture, test directory layout mirroring the source, and conventions for mocking and test data.

nodes.name: base.txt
nodes.summary: Core Python dependencies for OpenOutreach, including Playwright, Django, pydantic-ai (multi-provider LLM), fastembed, scikit-learn, and the openoutreach-cli package. Pins griffe<2 to avoid a pydantic-ai compatibility break.

nodes.name: crm.txt
nodes.summary: Placeholder requirements file for CRM-specific dependencies; currently empty (no extra packages beyond base).

nodes.name: local.txt
nodes.summary: Local development requirements extending base.txt with testing tools: pytest, pytest-django, pytest-mock, pytest-cov, and factory-boy.

nodes.name: production.txt
nodes.summary: Production requirements that simply include base.txt with no additional packages, keeping the production environment minimal.

nodes.name: entrypoint
nodes.summary: Docker entrypoint shell script that remaps the container's ubuntu UID/GID to match the host user (for local dev bind-mounts), ensures /app/data exists with correct ownership, then exec's the given command as the ubuntu user via gosu.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Docker start script that launches Xvfb virtual display (required for headed Playwright), optionally starts x11vnc and websockify for VNC remote access, then runs the Django daemon via `manage.py rundaemon`.

nodes.name: general.md
nodes.summary: General cross-language code style guide covering readability, consistency, simplicity, maintainability, and documentation principles for the project.

nodes.name: python.md
nodes.summary: Python-specific style guide summarising the Google Python Style Guide: linting, imports, type annotations, naming conventions (snake_case/PascalCase/ALL_CAPS), docstrings, 80-char line limit, and f-string usage.

nodes.name: .envrc
nodes.summary: direnv configuration file that activates the project's Python virtual environment automatically when entering the project directory.

nodes.name: ChatMessage (initial)
nodes.summary: Creates the ChatMessage table with a generic foreign key (content_type + object_id), owner/recipient M2M to auth.User, threading via answer_to and topic self-FKs, and a text content field.

nodes.name: ChatMessage (linkedin sync fields)
nodes.summary: Deletes legacy pre-sync ChatMessages, then adds is_outgoing (bool) and linkedin_urn (unique char, used for deduplication with Voyager API) fields to ChatMessage.

nodes.name: Lead + Deal (initial)
nodes.summary: Creates the Lead table (linkedin_url, public_identifier, embedding binary, disqualified flag) and the Deal table (state machine CharField, campaign FK, lead FK, connect_attempts, backoff_hours) with a unique constraint on (lead, campaign).

nodes.name: Lead.profile_data
nodes.summary: Adds a JSONField profile_data to Lead, migrates existing description text (parsed as JSON) into it, then removes the old description CharField.

nodes.name: Lead unique identifiers
nodes.summary: Backfills public_identifier from linkedin_url (and vice versa), fixes stale /in/me/ markers, then enforces unique + non-nullable constraints on both Lead.public_identifier and Lead.linkedin_url.

nodes.name: Deal.state (enums)
nodes.summary: Updates the Deal.state field choices to use the canonical ProfileState enum values from linkedin.enums.

nodes.name: Lead.urn
nodes.summary: Adds a nullable unique urn column to Lead and backfills it from profile_data['urn'], skipping collisions (same person under different public_identifiers).

nodes.name: Deal summaries
nodes.summary: Adds chat_summary and profile_summary nullable JSONField columns to Deal, used to store incremental mem0-style fact lists about the lead.

nodes.name: Lead cleanup
nodes.summary: Removes legacy first_name, last_name, company_name, and profile_data fields from Lead, completing the migration to fully scrape-based profile storage.

nodes.name: VACUUM
nodes.summary: Non-atomic migration that runs VACUUM on the SQLite database to reclaim space freed by the prior field-removal migration.

nodes.name: Deal.outcome
nodes.summary: Adds a Deal.outcome CharField with enum choices (converted/not_interested/wrong_fit/no_budget/has_solution/bad_timing/unresponsive/unknown), migrates data from the old closing_reason field, then drops closing_reason.

nodes.name: Deal.next_check_pending_at
nodes.summary: Adds indexed nullable DateTimeField next_check_pending_at to Deal, backfilling existing PENDING deals to now() so they are picked up immediately by the new scheduler.

nodes.name: Deal message approval
nodes.summary: Adds pending_message (TextField) and pending_message_approved (BooleanField) to Deal, enabling human-in-the-loop review of LLM-generated follow-up messages before sending.

nodes.name: __init__.py (chat/migrations)
nodes.summary: Empty Python package marker for the chat migrations module.

nodes.name: __init__.py (crm/migrations)
nodes.summary: Empty Python package marker for the crm migrations module.

nodes.name: django_settings.py
nodes.summary: Minimal Django settings module for OpenOutreach: SQLite database at data/db.sqlite3, installs crm/chat/linkedin apps, configures Django Admin, static/media roots, and sets DJANGO_ALLOW_ASYNC_UNSAFE for Playwright compatibility.

nodes.name: add_seeds.py
nodes.summary: Django management command that reads LinkedIn profile URLs from stdin and creates QUALIFIED seed Leads for a given campaign using the seeds setup module.

nodes.name: crm.py
nodes.summary: Django management command providing `manage.py crm` subcommands (leads, deals, deal <id>) to browse CRM data with Rich-formatted tables including state/outcome color coding and full deal detail with chat messages.

nodes.name: reset_data.py
nodes.summary: Django management command that deletes all Leads, Deals, and ActionLogs, resets SearchKeywords to unused, and clears Campaign ML model blobs — keeping Campaigns and LinkedInProfiles intact.

nodes.name: status.py
nodes.summary: Django management command that prints a system overview: active campaigns, deal counts by state per campaign, task queue breakdown by type/status, next scheduled task, and today's action counts.

nodes.name: Campaign + LinkedInProfile + Task + ActionLog + SearchKeyword (initial)
nodes.summary: Creates the core linkedin app tables: Campaign (with model_blob, users M2M), LinkedInProfile (credentials, limits, cookie_data), Task (type/status/payload/scheduled_at with index), ActionLog (connect/follow_up events), and SearchKeyword.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfile.self_lead
nodes.summary: Adds a nullable self_lead FK to LinkedInProfile (pointing to crm.Lead) to track the operator's own profile, and deletes legacy /in/me/ marker Lead rows.

nodes.name: SiteConfig
nodes.summary: Creates the SiteConfig singleton table (llm_api_key, ai_model, llm_api_base) and migrates existing .env LLM config values into the database, then deletes the .env file.

nodes.name: Campaign model_blob compression
nodes.summary: Data migration that re-serializes all existing Campaign.model_blob values with joblib zlib compression (level 3), achieving ~3-5x size reduction on GP pipeline objects.

nodes.name: Task.error removed
nodes.summary: Removes the error TextField from Task, simplifying the task model since error details are now logged elsewhere.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfile follow_up_daily_limit default
nodes.summary: Updates the default value of LinkedInProfile.follow_up_daily_limit from 30 to 25.

nodes.name: SiteConfig.llm_provider
nodes.summary: Adds llm_provider CharField to SiteConfig with choices for openai/anthropic/google/groq/mistral/cohere/openai_compatible; backfills existing rows with non-empty llm_api_base to 'openai_compatible' to preserve behaviour.

nodes.name: LinkedInProfile.connect_weekly_limit removed
nodes.summary: Removes connect_weekly_limit from LinkedInProfile; the daily planner now handles budget, and LinkedIn's own ceiling surfaces as a handler-level exception.

nodes.name: Drop stale PENDING Task rows
nodes.summary: Data migration that deletes all PENDING Task rows carrying the old per-lead payload format, allowing the new lazy Poisson-spaced planner to build a fresh queue on next daemon start.

nodes.name: .gitkeep (data/)
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file to ensure the data/ directory (which holds db.sqlite3 at runtime) is tracked by git.

nodes.name: __init__.py (linkedin/migrations)
nodes.summary: Empty Python package marker for the linkedin migrations module.

nodes.name: to_translate.txt
nodes.summary: Django i18n translation template stub listing business-domain strings (industry types, deal stages, outcome reasons) that need localisation via the {% translate %} tag.

nodes.name: 0010_remove_freemium_newsletter.py
nodes.summary: Django migration that removes the freemium_newsletter field from the Campaign model, with a dependency on migration 0009.

nodes.name: 0011_campaign_website_url_linkedinprofile_contact_email.py
nodes.summary: Django migration adding website_url to Campaign and contact_email to LinkedInProfile models, enabling campaign-level website tracking and per-profile contact email storage.

nodes.name: 0012_message_approval.py
nodes.summary: Django migration adding message approval fields to the Deal model, supporting the human-in-the-loop approval workflow for outgoing LinkedIn messages.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the linkedin.ml ML sub-package.

nodes.name: profile_text.py
nodes.summary: Provides the build_profile_text utility that serializes a Voyager LinkedIn profile dict into a single lowercased string suitable for embedding by the ML pipeline.

nodes.name: build_profile_text
nodes.summary: Converts a Voyager profile dict into a normalized, space-joined text string by extracting headline, industry, position history, and education entries.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the linkedin.pipeline sub-package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the linkedin.setup sub-package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the linkedin.templates sub-package.

nodes.name: follow_up_agent.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 system prompt for the follow-up LLM agent; instructs the model to act as a named seller using the Mom Test discovery/pitching framework, with strict identity binding to prevent seller/lead confusion and action-selection logic for send_message, wait, or mark_completed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 template variables for self_name, product_docs, profile_summary, chat_summary, recent_messages, and contact_email. Identity binding via self_name is critical to prevent the LLM from misattributing seller references in lead replies.

nodes.name: qualify_lead.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 prompt template instructing the LLM to evaluate a LinkedIn profile as a B2B prospect by assessing role, industry, decision-making authority, and company fit against campaign objectives.

nodes.name: search_keywords.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 prompt template that instructs the LLM to generate LinkedIn People search keyword queries for a campaign, with deduplication against previously-used keywords.

nodes.name: urls.py
nodes.summary: Root Django URL configuration mapping the /admin/ path to Django Admin and serving static media files.

nodes.name: manage.py
nodes.summary: Django management entrypoint that defaults to the rundaemon command when invoked with no subcommand, enabling the project to self-start the automation daemon without arguments.
nodes.languageNotes: Defaults sys.argv to ['rundaemon'] when no subcommand is provided, so `python manage.py` starts the daemon directly.

nodes.name: oo_cli.py
nodes.summary: Typer-based CLI providing the full `oo` command set for managing the automation daemon, Django admin, CRM (leads, deals, pending approval, state/outcome mutations), campaigns, tasks, and search keywords with Rich-formatted table output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Typer sub-apps (crm_app, campaign_app, task_app, keyword_app) to organize commands. Rich console and Table are used for all output rendering.

nodes.name: status
nodes.summary: Renders a system overview showing campaign count, deal state breakdown, task queue summary, next scheduled task, and today's action log counts per account.

nodes.name: crm_leads
nodes.summary: Lists all leads (optionally filtered to disqualified) in a Rich table showing ID, LinkedIn identifier, URL, disqualified flag, and creation date.

nodes.name: crm_deals
nodes.summary: Lists deals with optional state/campaign filters in a Rich table, rendering state and outcome with color-coded badges.

nodes.name: crm_deal
nodes.summary: Displays full detail for a single deal including state, outcome, profile/chat summaries, and the full chat message thread formatted as a conversation.

nodes.name: crm_pending
nodes.summary: Lists deals with pending outgoing messages awaiting approval, showing lead name, state, profile summary bullet, and the drafted message.

nodes.name: crm_approve
nodes.summary: Approves a pending outgoing LinkedIn message on a deal by setting the approved_message field and clearing the pending draft.

nodes.name: crm_reject
nodes.summary: Rejects a pending outgoing LinkedIn message on a deal, clearing both pending and approved message fields.

nodes.name: crm_set_state
nodes.summary: Manually sets a deal's state to one of the valid ProfileState values, with case-insensitive matching and rich color output.

nodes.name: crm_set_outcome
nodes.summary: Manually sets a deal's outcome to one of the valid Outcome enum values, with case-insensitive matching.

nodes.name: campaign_create
nodes.summary: Creates a new Campaign record and automatically associates all existing LinkedIn user accounts to it.

nodes.name: campaign_update
nodes.summary: Updates one or more campaign fields (objective, booking_link, docs, website, name, require_approval) and reports which fields changed.

nodes.name: task_list
nodes.summary: Lists scheduled tasks with status/limit filters in a Rich table, resolving campaign names from a pre-fetched map and formatting scheduled_at timestamps.

nodes.name: keyword_list
nodes.summary: Lists search keywords with optional campaign filter in a Rich table, showing ID, campaign, keyword, used_at timestamp, and enabled status.

nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the tests root package.

nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the tests/agents test sub-package.

nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the tests/browser test sub-package.

nodes.name: test_lazy_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Django TestCase suite verifying the lazy Voyager-scraping accessors on the Lead model: get_profile (always live), get_urn (cached column with scrape fallback), and get_embedding (cached bytes with enrichment fallback).

nodes.name: TestGetProfile
nodes.summary: Tests that Lead.get_profile always performs a live Voyager scrape, populates the urn column as a side effect, returns None on empty responses, and propagates API exceptions.

nodes.name: TestGetUrn
nodes.summary: Tests that Lead.get_urn reads from the cached DB column without scraping when populated, and falls back to a scrape when the column is null.

nodes.name: TestGetEmbedding
nodes.summary: Tests that Lead.get_embedding returns the cached numpy array without scraping, performs a full enrich-and-embed cycle when the cache is cold, and propagates API errors.

nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file to preserve the tests/fixtures/pages directory in version control for storing HTML page snapshots used in browser tests.

nodes.name: linkedin_profile.json
nodes.summary: Large LinkedIn Voyager API response fixture with data and included arrays, used as a realistic profile payload in ML and enrichment tests.

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: test_embeddings.py

layers.name: Browser Automation
layers.description: Playwright stealth browser session management, LinkedIn login, human-rhythm navigation, and LinkedIn UI interaction actions (connect, message, search, status, conversations, profile scraping)

layers.name: API Integration
layers.description: LinkedIn Voyager API client, response parsing, and messaging REST/GraphQL endpoints for profile retrieval, conversation fetch, and message sending

layers.name: Automation Core
layers.description: Task queue daemon, Poisson-spaced scheduler, task handlers (connect, check_pending, follow_up), diagnostics, and onboarding/setup bootstrap for the automation engine

layers.name: ML Pipeline
layers.description: Bayesian GPR qualifier with BALD active learning, fastembed text embeddings, LLM-powered lead qualification and follow-up agent, pipeline orchestration (search, qualify, pools), and Jinja2 prompt templates

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Django ORM models (Campaign, Lead, Deal, Task, ChatMessage, SiteConfig), database access functions for deals/leads/chat, state machine enums, migrations for all three Django apps, and custom exception types

layers.name: CRM & Admin
layers.description: Django Admin registrations for Lead, Deal, Campaign, Task, and ChatMessage with colored badges, pipeline stats, conversation thread rendering, and the oo CLI interface

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Django settings, project-wide constants (active hours, ML defaults, browser timeouts), timezone detection, logging setup, Python packaging, dependency manifests, and Docker build configuration

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker multi-stage build, container entrypoint/start scripts for Xvfb and VNC, and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines for testing and deploying to ghcr.io

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest test suite covering browser selectors, Voyager API parsing, ML qualifier, pipeline pools, scheduler, task handlers, DB layer, agent prompts, and shared fixtures/factories

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README, architecture reference, API docs, GDPR roadmap, Docker guide, follow-up agent docs, product guidelines, tech-stack spec, and Conductor workflow/planning documents

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand OpenOutreach's core value proposition: a self-hosted LinkedIn automation tool that autonomously discovers, qualifies, and contacts B2B leads without requiring a pre-built list. The README explains the five-step pipeline — from product description through AI-generated search queries, Bayesian ML qualification, automated connection, and multi-turn follow-up — and serves as the roadmap for the entire tour. The architecture overview at the top of ARCHITECTURE.md expands on each component in detail, making it the ideal companion reference as you explore the code.

tour.title: Entry Point and Daemon Bootstrap
tour.description: The project self-starts with a single command: `manage.py` defaults to `rundaemon` when invoked with no arguments. The `rundaemon` management command performs the full bootstrap sequence — running migrations, triggering onboarding if configuration is missing, creating the AccountSession, and handing control to `daemon.py`. The daemon is the central coordinator: it claims tasks from the queue, dispatches them to typed handlers, manages active-hours scheduling, handles browser crashes and authentication failures, and runs the scheduler reconciliation loop on every idle cycle. With fan-out of 9 (the highest in the codebase), daemon.py touches nearly every subsystem.
tour.languageLesson: Django management commands live under `management/commands/` and subclass `BaseCommand`. The `rundaemon` command calls `migrate` programmatically before starting the automation loop, ensuring the database schema is always up to date before any DB access.

tour.title: Configuration Layer
tour.description: All tunable constants are concentrated in two places: `conf.py` (fan-in 14, imported by every subsystem) holds hardcoded defaults for browser timeouts, active-hours scheduling, Poisson planner caps, and ML thresholds; `django_settings.py` provides the minimal Django configuration — SQLite at `data/db.sqlite3`, the three installed apps, and `DJANGO_ALLOW_ASYNC_UNSAFE` to let Playwright run inside the synchronous ORM context. Understanding these two files tells you what every dial does before diving into the code that reads them.

tour.title: Django Data Models
tour.description: Three Django apps form the persistence layer. The `linkedin` app (`linkedin/models.py`) holds the operational models — Campaign (with its binary ML model blob), LinkedInProfile (credentials and rate-limit state), SearchKeyword, ActionLog, and the Task queue. The `crm` app splits Lead and Deal into separate model files: Lead stores a prospect's identity and 384-dim embedding; Deal tracks that prospect's pipeline progression with state, outcome, LLM summaries, and pending message drafts. The `chat` app provides ChatMessage via a GenericForeignKey so conversation history can attach to any object. Together these five tables define the entire data model.
tour.languageLesson: Django's GenericForeignKey (content_type + object_id) lets ChatMessage attach to any model without a fixed FK. This pattern is useful when a single table must relate to multiple parent models — here it allows future attachment of messages to objects beyond Lead.

tour.title: State Machine and Enums
tour.description: The entire lead lifecycle is governed by `ProfileState`, a `StrEnum` in `linkedin/enums.py` (fan-in 16 — the second-most-imported file in the codebase). A Lead's Deal transitions through QUALIFIED → READY_TO_CONNECT → PENDING → CONNECTED → COMPLETED or FAILED; the `Outcome` enum on Deal records why a deal terminated. This file is intentionally tiny — it is the shared vocabulary that every layer (models, tasks, pipeline, admin) imports, so keeping it minimal prevents circular dependencies. The companion doc `docs/profile_lifecycle.md` provides the full state diagram with transition triggers.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `StrEnum` (available from 3.11, back-ported via `enum.StrEnum`) makes enum members compare equal to their string values, so `deal.state == 'QUALIFIED'` works without an explicit `.value` access. This is the idiomatic choice for Django `CharField` choices where the DB stores strings.

tour.title: Browser Automation Core
tour.description: Browser automation is built in three layers. `session.py` wraps a Playwright browser instance and a LinkedInProfile into an `AccountSession`, providing lazy browser launch, cookie refresh, and reauthentication. `login.py` handles the actual stealth launch — human-typed credentials, comply-gate dismissal, and cookie persistence. `nav.py` (fan-in 6) is the foundation everything else builds on: `goto_page` with URL verification, `human_type` for keystroke-rate randomization, and `find_top_card` for profile page detection. Every UI action (connect, message, search) imports `nav.py` and runs through this shared layer.
tour.languageLesson: Playwright's `page.evaluate()` runs JavaScript in the browser process. OpenOutreach uses this to execute LinkedIn's Voyager API fetch() calls inside the real browser context so all cookies and headers are automatically attached — a cleaner approach than trying to replicate session state externally.

tour.title: LinkedIn Voyager API
tour.description: LinkedIn exposes an undocumented internal REST/GraphQL API called Voyager. `api/client.py` (fan-in 4) is the core authenticated client — it fires requests via `page.evaluate()` inside the Playwright browser so every call carries the real browser's session cookies. `api/voyager.py` is a pure parsing layer: it defines dataclasses for profiles, positions, and educations, and implements `parse_linkedin_voyager_response()` which resolves URN-reference chains embedded in the API's `included` array. The `api/messaging/` subpackage adds GraphQL conversation fetching and REST message sending. This API layer is the project's single source of truth for profile data.

tour.title: Task System and Scheduler
tour.description: The task queue is built on a `Task` Django model (persistent, survives restarts) with three task types: `connect`, `check_pending`, and `follow_up`. Tasks are intentionally lazy — they carry only a `campaign_id` payload, and handlers resolve the actual target at execution time via an eligibility query, avoiding staleness. `scheduler.py` (fan-in 3) is the planning engine: `reconcile()` runs on every daemon idle cycle, re-planning any task type whose pending queue is empty using Poisson-spaced slots spread over a 24-hour working window. The `working_seconds_in_window` and `poisson_slot_times` primitives implement uniform order-statistic spacing so actions appear human-paced.
tour.languageLesson: Poisson process sampling via uniform order statistics: to place n events over a window [0,T], draw n uniform samples and sort them. This gives the same joint distribution as a Poisson process with rate n/T, but guarantees exactly n events — making it predictable for daily rate-limit enforcement while still looking random.

tour.title: ML Pipeline and Qualification
tour.description: The qualification pipeline has two tightly coupled layers. The `BayesianQualifier` in `ml/qualifier.py` (fan-in 6) implements Gaussian Process Regression with BALD acquisition — it balances exploiting known-good profiles against exploring uncertain ones to learn the ideal customer profile. The pipeline orchestration in `pipeline/pools.py` (fan-out 6) chains composable generators: `search_source → qualify_source → ready_source → find_candidate`, with adaptive exploit/explore thresholds. `pipeline/qualify.py` is the step that calls the LLM (via the Jinja2 prompt in `qualify_lead.j2`) and persists the decision as a qualified or disqualified Deal. This combination means the model improves with every campaign run.
tour.languageLesson: BALD (Bayesian Active Learning by Disagreement) selects the sample that would most reduce model uncertainty. In a GPR context this is the point with highest predictive variance — profiles the model is most unsure about. Labeling these first yields a better model faster than random selection.

tour.title: Follow-Up Agent
tour.description: Once a lead is CONNECTED, the follow-up task handler drives multi-turn conversations through `agents/follow_up.py`. The agent reads the lead's `profile_summary` (a mem0-style JSON fact list built from a Voyager scrape) and `chat_summary` (incrementally updated with each message sync) alongside the last six chat messages, then produces a structured `FollowUpDecision` — send a message, mark completed, or wait. The LLM bridge in `llm.py` routes calls to OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Groq via pydantic-ai and runs them on a persistent worker thread to avoid event-loop conflicts with Playwright. The Jinja2 system prompt in `follow_up_agent.j2` implements the Mom Test framework and includes strict seller/lead identity binding to prevent the LLM from confusing the two parties.

tour.title: CRM and Admin Interface
tour.description: Django Admin is the primary operator interface. `crm/admin.py` (fan-out 4) registers Lead and Deal with colored state/outcome badges, conversation bubble rendering for chat threads, and inline ChatMessage display. `linkedin/admin.py` covers the operational models — Campaign with pipeline state pills, LinkedInProfile, Task queue, SearchKeyword, and ActionLog. The `oo_cli.py` Typer CLI provides a terminal-friendly alternative: `oo crm leads/deals`, `oo crm approve/reject` for human-in-the-loop message approval, and `oo campaign/task/keyword` management. Both interfaces expose the same underlying Django ORM queries.

tour.title: Test Suite and Infrastructure
tour.description: The test suite mirrors the source tree under `tests/`, with `tests/conftest.py` providing two autouse fixtures: CRM bootstrap (ensures the Django Site object exists) and an embedding stub (replaces fastembed with zeros to keep tests fast). `tests/factories.py` provides Factory Boy factories for User, Lead, and Deal. Key test areas covered: Voyager API parsing (`tests/api/test_voyager.py`), the BayesianQualifier (`tests/ml/test_qualifier.py`), scheduler timing and Poisson arithmetic (`tests/test_scheduler_advanced.py`, `tests/test_reconcile.py`), and browser selector regression tests against saved HTML fixtures (`tests/browser/test_connect_selectors.py`). The Dockerfile uses a two-stage build — a `deps` stage with uv for fast Python installs and a `runtime` stage with Playwright Chromium and optional VNC — and CI runs the full suite via `make docker-test` on every push.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM statements. The deps stage has build tools and the full requirements, while the runtime stage copies only the installed packages — keeping the production image lean. For Playwright, the runtime stage must also run `playwright install` to download the browser binary.
